composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and enhance the backend code in app.py
- Ensure the Flask app correctly locates and renders all HTML templates by verifying the 'templates' directory structure and paths.
- Review and fix any routing or redirect logic that may cause unexpected HTTP 302 responses.
- Maintain all existing backend functionalities including routing for all ten frontend pages, file read/write operations on the 'data' directory text files, and business logic for browsing pets, adoption applications, favorites, messaging, user profile, and admin panel.
- Coordinate with Programmer 2 to confirm that template file names and route URLs match exactly to avoid TemplateNotFound errors.
composition.Programmer 2:
Implement and organize all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Develop and place all ten HTML templates (dashboard.html, pet_listings.html, pet_details.html, add_pet.html, adoption_application.html, my_applications.html, favorites.html, messages.html, user_profile.html, admin_panel.html) inside the 'templates' folder at the root level of the project, alongside app.py.
- Ensure that the templates strictly follow the detailed page design and element IDs as specified in the requirements document.
- Use Jinja2 templating syntax to dynamically render data passed from the backend.
- Verify that all URLs in the templates correspond exactly to the Flask routes defined in app.py.
- Coordinate with Programmer 1 to ensure template file names and route URLs are consistent to prevent TemplateNotFound errors.
